name: Jumper Wires
fullName: Dupont Jumper Wires Set (M-M, M-F, F-F)
tagline: Flexible color-coded wires for connecting components on a breadboard or to Arduino pins
description: Jumper wires (Dupont wires) are pre-made flexible cables used to make electrical connections between components on a breadboard and Arduino/microcontroller pins. They come in three types: Male-to-Male (M-M) for breadboard connections, Male-to-Female (M-F) for connecting to Arduino headers, and Female-to-Female (F-F) for sensor modules. Color coding helps keep wiring organized.

pinout.name: Male–Male
pinout.desc: Both ends have pins — for breadboard-to-breadboard connections

pinout.name: Male–Female
pinout.desc: One pin, one socket — for breadboard-to-Arduino header

pinout.name: Female–Female
pinout.desc: Both ends have sockets — for module pin-to-pin connections

wiringNote: Use red wires for VCC/power, black or brown for GND, and other colors for signal. This color convention prevents wiring mistakes.
commonMistakes: Using damaged or loose connectors → intermittent connections that are hard to debug
Not following color conventions → wrong connections cause shorts or sensor damage
Overcrowding a breadboard with too many wires → spaghetti wiring, hard to trace
Forcing a wire into a damaged breadboard hole → connector breaks
